answer: In Manusmriti chapter 8, the penalty for a non-Brahmin (low-born man) who strikes a Brahmin (a superior) is the mutilation of the very limb used to commit the assault. The general rule (Mn_8.279) states: 'With whatever limb a low-born man should injure a superior, that very limb of his shall be cut off; this is the ordinance of Manu.' The specific application (Mn_8.280) elaborates: 'He who raises his hand or a stick deserves to have his hand cut off; he who strikes in anger with his foot deserves to have his foot cut off.' In short, the precise penalty is amputation of the offending limb — hand (if struck with hand or stick) or foot (if struck with foot). Work slug: unknown-manusmrti-347b76, chapter 8, verses Mn_8.279–Mn_8.280.
